title: corpus_manifest
description: Phase 4 corpus manifest. Single source of truth for the 13-transcript corpus. The `declared` block is hand-maintained by the operator. The `observed` block is overwritten by `spectrum-core ingest-corpus` on every run; manual edits to `observed` are not respected. `manifest_hash` is a sha256 of the canonicalized sources array (sorted by source_id, then field-sorted) computed at write time and verified at load time; mismatch fails closed with reason `corpus_manifest_hash_mismatch`. Uniqueness of `source_id` and `expected_path` plus the `supersedes` cross-reference are enforced by `spectrum_systems_core.corpus.manifest_loader` (JSON Schema cannot natively express them).

manifest_hash.description: sha256 hex digest over the canonicalized sources array (sorted by source_id, then field-sorted within each entry). Loader recomputes and asserts equality on read. The placeholder `PLACEHOLDER_HASH_REGENERATED_BY_LOADER` is accepted by the schema but rejected by the loader's hash gate — operators must regenerate the hash by running the manifest_loader's recompute helper before committing.

expected_path.description: Path to the raw transcript, relative to the data lake root. Loader rejects empty / absolute paths.

meeting_date.description: ISO-8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). Time zones not encoded — the corpus is a list of transcripts, not a schedule.

supersedes.description: Either null or the source_id of an earlier entry this one supersedes. Cross-reference (must exist in the same manifest) is verified by the loader.
